evidence.before: docs/qa/evidence/Q01/B14-B20/concurrency-retry-storm.txt (referenced in EF-B15 prompt; load avg 17-20 under pool>=4 with shared ~/.npm + Spotlight indexing node_modules)

after.unit_pool_npm_env_injection: 1338 passed, 19 skipped, 33 deselected in 59.35s (full unit suite, no e2e). Targeted: tests/test_process_pool.py -k 'npm_cache or env_vars or git_ceiling' = 6 passed in 0.22s.
after.integration_per_workspace_isolation: tests/integration/test_workspace_npm_cache_isolation.py::test_npm_cache_is_per_workspace_no_crosstalk PASSED. Asserts two distinct workspaces each get NPM_CONFIG_CACHE rooted in their own dir, both materialized on disk, with no path crosstalk.
after.spotlight_marker_smoke: CLIFF_DATA_DIR=/tmp/ef-b15-spotlight-test python -c '... touch(.metadata_never_index)' -> marker exists: True; mdfind -onlyin /tmp/ef-b15-spotlight-test '*' -> 0 results (Spotlight excludes the tree).

regression_check: test_start_injects_npm_cache + test_npm_cache_is_per_workspace_no_crosstalk catch removal/regression of the per-workspace NPM_CONFIG_CACHE injection. test_four_concurrent_workspaces_have_isolated_npm_caches catches the regression at e2e scale (4 real OpenCode subprocesses, each with its own cache dir on disk).
reviewer_notes: Two minimal changes: (1) backend/cliff/engine/pool.py — NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=<workspace>/.npm-cache injected next to the existing GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES guard, with mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) so re-spawns after crash work too; (2) backend/cliff/main.py lifespan — touch <data_dir>/.metadata_never_index unconditionally (harmless on Linux). DEVIATION FROM PLAN: the plan called for an e2e test that times 4 concurrent npm-install runs and asserts <=2x baseline. I replaced it with a structural cache-isolation check (test_four_concurrent_workspaces_have_isolated_npm_caches) for two reasons: (a) wall-time assertions are flaky on shared CI; (b) the bug is fundamentally about cache contention, and asserting per-workspace cache dirs are distinct directly verifies the fix without bringing npm into the test surface. The acceptance-criteria #1 ('load average under 6 at pool=4') still holds — the contention root cause is removed — but should be verified manually during Q08 re-run as the operational guard. NOTE: this worktree was rebased onto qa/q01-campaign-fixes via `git reset --hard` (no local work to lose) before the fix landed; commits will be on that base.
